title: Domestic Violence Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Domestic Violence Support Groups 40 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Hispanic/Latino Community LGBTQ2+ Issues Mothers ABUSE RECOVERY MINISTRY AND SERVICES HER JOURNEY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 15 week faith-based program that provides a safe and supportive environment for women seeking healing from current or past abusive or controlling relationships. Individuals can join at any time and continue as long as they find it helpful. Groups are held in multiple locations in person and virtually. Eligibility: Women age 14 and older who have experienced domestic abuse, including emotional, verbal, sexual, spiritual, financial, animal, property, and/or physical abuse Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 846-9284 Visit Website More Details BRADLEY ANGLE HEALING ROOTS & KINSHIP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers culturally specific advocacy and support groups. Staff connect survivors to resources for housing, employment, education, medical care, legal assistance, and other services. Helps survivors learn more about domestic violence, get support from staff and peers, and create social connections within an affirming and welcoming space. Eligibility: Black and African-American survivors of domestic violence Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm, appointment required Email (503) 232-1528 311 Visit Website More Details BRADLEY ANGLE MARTHA'S FOLX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers confidential services such as support groups, resource referrals, safety planning, shelter and housing assistance, and basic needs assistance. Also offers related classes and workshops. Eligibility: Self-identified LGBTQ2+ folks of all genders who have experienced or are experiencing intimate partner violence Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm; by appointment only Email (503) 232-1528 305 Visit Website More Details CENTER AGAINST RAPE AND DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides sexual assault and domestic violence support groups. 2208 SW 3rd Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Meeting times vary Phone hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 754-0110 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR HOPE AND SAFETY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of domestic violence, sexual assault, and human trafficking survivor support services such as support groups, 911 cell phones, court accompaniment, community education, the Address Confidentiality Program, and information and referral to other social services. 605 Center Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual abuse, human trafficking, and/or stalking, regardless of gender identity or sexual orientation Hours: Walk in: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Phone: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 399-7722 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS BEHAVORIAL HEALTH CENTER FOR FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health counseling that includes but is not limited to individual and family therapy, career counseling, academic school guidance, domestic violence (individual counseling and support groups), and new mother support. Also provides foster parent support and adult foster care support (life skills, personal and social development, conflict management, and budgeting). Intensive Outpatient Services (IOP) include medication management, individual therapy, and family/couples therapy as needed. Most services are provided virtually. 2665 Villa Creek Drive Farmers Branch, TX 75234 Eligibility: -Varies by service; most programs are for ages 3-19 and their adult caregivers -Adult Foster Care and Group Home Support: Individuals with complex health needs who are in a home-like or residential setting -IOP: Individuals transitioning back to work or school or who need extra support beyond an outpatient appointment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (469) 343-4081 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS PORTLAND BEHAVORIAL HEALTH CENTER FOR FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health counseling that includes but is not limited to individual and family therapy, career counseling, academic school guidance, domestic violence (individual counseling and support groups), and new mother support. Also provides foster parent support and adult foster care support (life skills, personal and social development, conflict management, and budgeting). Intensive Outpatient Services (IOP) include medication management, individual therapy, and family/couples therapy as needed. Most services are provided virtually. 1340 SW Bertha Boulevard Suite 201 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: -Varies by service; most programs are for ages 3-19 and their adult caregivers -Adult Foster Care and Group Home Support: Individuals with complex health needs who are in a home-like or residential setting -IOP: Individuals transitioning back to work or school or who need extra support beyond an outpatient appointment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 894-7026 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS WOMEN'S SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, confidential text and chat line, emergency shelter and transitional housing services, legal advocacy to assist with temporary restraining orders, safety planning, housing advocacy and financial empowerment programs, violence prevention, and community education. 256 Warner Milne Road Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault or sex industry, and older adults experiencing elder abuse Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other assistance: Varies by service Email (888) 654-2288 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WORKS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Advocacy for survivors of domestic violence including safety planning, resource navigation, emotional support and systems advocacy including support groups. 2594 E Barnett Road Suite C Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Any individual experiencing domestic violence including stalking Hours: Helpline: 24 hours a day / 7 days a week Email (541) 779-4357 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS GOVERNMENT OFFICE CIRCLES OF HEALING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy, information and referrals, support groups, and a crisis line. 1245 Fulton Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and intimate partner violence, sexual assault, stalking, and human trafficking. Hours: Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 808-8152 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONSEJO HISPANO MUJERES APOYANDO MUJERES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A support group providing a safe space to talk with other women. Topics include healthy relationships and domestic violence. Zumba is offered with a certified instructor. Snacks, refreshments, and childcare are provided during the group. 565 12th Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as female and their children Hours: Thursday 5:30pm-7:30pm Bob Chisholm Community Center 1225 Avenue A Seaside, OR 97138 Email (503) 325-4547 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC BOARDMAN ADVOCACY CENTER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer domestic violence and sexual abuse support groups, including bilingual support groups. 200 City Center Circle Boardman, OR 97818 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday 1pm-5pm by appointment only (541) 567-0424 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC HERMISTON ADVOCACY CENTER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer domestic violence and sexual abuse support groups, including bilingual support groups. 1050 W Elm Avenue Suite 250 Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 567-0424 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC IONE ADVOCACY CENTER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer domestic violence and sexual abuse support groups, including bilingual support groups. 365 W 3rd Street Ione, OR 97843 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday 9:30am-3:30pm by appointment only (541) 276-3322 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC MILTON-FREEWATER ADVOCACY CENTER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer domestic violence and sexual abuse support groups, including bilingual support groups. 722 S Main Street Milton Freewater, OR 97862 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Thursday 1pm-5pm by appointment only (541) 276-3322 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC PENDLETON ADVOCACY CENTER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer domestic violence and sexual abuse support groups, including bilingual support groups. 1103 SE Court Place Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 276-3322 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy, protective order assistance, emergency shelter, safety planning, court and medical accompaniment, referrals, support groups, and a crisis line. 726 N 1st Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, dating violence, human trafficking, and elder abuse. Also serves individulas with disabilities who are victims of abuse. Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 947-2449 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO UNICA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a crisis line for domestic violence and sexual assault. Also provides case management, survivor advocacy, housing help, support groups, parenting support, community education, and help with restraining and stalking orders. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Hispanic and Latino domestic violence and sexual assault survivors Hours: Crisis Line: 24 hours a day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 232-4448 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offer support by providing information and helping with forms related to safety concerns. Assist with crisis planning, restraining orders, crime reporting, mental health counseling, support groups, and cultural advocacy. Provides Safety and Stabilization Assessment (SSA) for domestic violence shelters or housing resources. SSA is required before accessing most direct domestic violence services. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Survivors of intimate partner violence and/or sexual assult Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, safety planning, short-term shelter, counseling, court advocacy, support groups, and referral to and advocacy with local agencies and other programs that offer long-term shelter. For survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault, provides assistance with temporary restraining and stalking orders. Does not provide intervention services for perpetrators of violence. 420 E 3rd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week -Hours vary for other services Email (541) 296-1662 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEART OF GRANT COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, short-term emergency shelter, confidential walk-in center, peer counseling, transportation, temporary safe housing and access to transitional housing, support groups, medical and legal advocacy, outreach and education regarding domestic violence and sexual assault. 115 NW Bridge Street John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault Hours: Crisis line and shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm and by appointment Email (541) 620-1342 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS AGAINST VIOLENCE HOTLINE AND EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis hotline and a 30-day emergency shelter with six rooms for survivors of sexual assault, domestic violence, or stalking. Offers medical and legal advocacy, including accompaniment to appointments and assistance with paperwork. Food, clothing, and peer counseling are available as well. PO Box 441 Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Anyone fleeing sexual assault, domestic violence, or stalking Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 386-6603 247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HENDERSON HOUSE HENDERSON HOUSE ADVOCACY CENTER CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides domestic violence and sexual assault client advocacy services. Services include a crisis hotline, coordinated wraparound service, referrals, assistance with filling out protective orders, youth outreach support groups, weekly support groups for domestic violence and sexual violence survivors (including adult survivors of child sexual abuse), and an art therapy group. 610 SE 1st Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Survivors of interpersonal and/or sexual violence (including stalking), human trafficking, and their children who have experienced or witnessed abuse Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Support groups: Tuesday/ Thursday 6pm-7:30pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 472-1503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOPE AND SAFETY ALLIANCE CRISIS LINE AND SUPPORT CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential crisis and support line for safety planning and referrals, a safe house, support groups, and community education to prevent intimate partner violence. Can assist with restraining orders and accompanying individuals to the Lane County Courthouse. Rural outreach provided by arrangement. 1577 Pearl Street Floor 2, Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Survivors of intimate partner violence of any kind, regardless of gender, as well as their families and friends Hours: Crisis and Support Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Web Chat: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Appointments: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 485-6513 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY SAFE HOUSE ALLIANCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a range of advocacy and support to survivors of domestic violence, elder abuse, dating violence, trafficking, or sexual assault. Services include crisis intervention, court and medical accompaniment, peer advocacy, support groups, assistance with locating financial help, assistance with restraining and stalking orders, community education, referrals to meet emergency needs, and emergency shelter. 103 S Kerby Avenue Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, elder abuse, dating violence, trafficking, or sexual assault Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, closed federal holidays Email (541) 592-2515 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER PARENT CHILD THERAPEUTIC SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers therapeutic services to domestic violence survivors and their children. Services are intended to strengthen the parent-child relationship, provide support with behavioral issues, build stress management skills, support emotional regulation, and help survivors access additional community resources. Parent-Child Specialists also support caregivers in understanding how domestic violence and trauma impact children and the parent-child relationship. Services can be offered in the home, shelter, or Gateway Center for Domestic Violence and are generally offered weekly for at least a few months. Also provides a parenting support group for mothers who are survivors. 7211 SE 62nd Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Families that have experienced domestic violence (physical, verbal, and/or emotional abuse), have at least one child between the ages of 0-12, and are not living with the abusive person Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Closed Friday, December 22, 2023-Monday, January 1, 2024 Email (503) 568-1720 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a helpline, support groups, and a variety of supportive services for survivors of intimate partner violence, sexual assault, stalking, and trafficking. Provides emergency shelter services, based on availability, for survivors. Also provides in-person response to the emergency room for sexual assault survivors and assistance with protective and stalking orders. Every person who contacts Klamath Advocacy Center gets five essential services for safety and independence: safety plan, risk assessment, needs assessment, resource information, and next steps. PO Box 1358 Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Shelter: Women and women with minor children who are survivors of domestic/intimate partner violence or sexual assault All other services: Survivors of domestic/intimate partner violence, sexual assault, dating violence, and/or stalking Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 884-0390 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HEALING CIRCLE DOMESTIC VIOLENCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides domestic and sexual violence services, including safety planning, crisis intervention, advocacy, and educational services. Works with local programs to facilitate safety planning, provide transportation as necessary, and ensure the delivery of culturally relevant services in local shelters. Weekly domestic violence support groups that are closed groups held off-site for confidentiality reasons. Free child care is available. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence, primarily serves those who self-identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm. Support group times vary. Email (503) 288-8177 339 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PEACE AT HOME ADVOCACY CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SHELTER AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, emergency shelter, support groups, and legal advocacy (assistance with protective and stalking orders). 1202 SE Douglas Avenue Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Individuals and families experiencing domestic abuse, sexual assault, human trafficking, or stalking. Shelter allows pets. Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 673-7867 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SABLE HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers case management, safety planning, information and referral, advocacy, support groups, community outreach, and education related to domestic and sexual violence. Support groups are available both to the public and to residents of the confidential shelter facility. Also provides rental and housing assistance services. 314 SE Oak Street Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic or sexual violence and their children Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 623-6703 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support groups and community education on sexual harassment, sexual assault, and domestic violence and adult domestic violence prevention workshops. Provides medical, legal, and financial advocacy for survivors such as navigating the Department of Human Services Child Welfare and the Self Sufficiency office. Assists with applying for Temporary Assistance for Domestic Violence Survivors (TA-DVS). Rural outreach: Supports survivors with safety planning, support groups, and meets for weekly consultation. 2194 Columbia Boulevard Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and/or sexual violence, stalking, and sex trafficking Hours: Varies Email (503) 397-6161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE PROJECT THE SAFE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shelter for domestic violence and sexual assault survivors. Also provides a crisis line, outreach services, support groups, and community education. 1681 Newmark Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Coos Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Curry Outreach Office Tuesday - Friday 8am to 4pm Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 756-7000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. 990 NW Brooks Street Suite 1 Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 389-7021 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE MADRAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 475-1880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE PRINEVILLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 416-2114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE REDMOND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 504-2550 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SELF ENHANCEMENT INC SELF ENHANCEMENT INC COMMUNITY & FAMILY PROGRAMS ANTI DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers safe and confidential services to survivors of domestic and sexual violence including advocacy, case management, support groups, safety planning, relocation assistance, emergency, medical and judicial accompaniments, and referrals to other resources. 310 NE Failing Street Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week. Calls placed outside of Monday-Friday 9am-5pm and holidays are forwarded to Call to Safety. Email (503) 972-3698 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHELTER FROM THE STORM LA GRANDE - SHELTER FROM THE STORM DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a crisis line and emergency shelter. Advocacy includes information and referral, assistance with restraining orders, reporting options, counseling and support groups, and other supportive services. 10901 Island Avenue Island City, OR 97850 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence or sexual assault and their families and supporters Hours: Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 963-7226 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE SALVATION ARMY WEST WOMEN'S AND CHILDREN'S SHELTER WEST WOMEN'S AND CHILDREN’S SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to those escaping domestic violence. Crisis intervention services include emergency shelter, crisis line, advocacy, therapeutic children's programs, and support groups. Self-sufficiency programming includes transitional housing and advocacy, information and referral, and life skills training. Provides community education and educational materials. The program is 60 days with the potential of an extension to 90 days. Eligibility: Single women and women with children (male children must be under age 18) fleeing domestic violence, sexual assault, trafficking, intimate partner violence, or elder abuse Hours: Shelter hours and crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Client screenings: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 731-3900 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TIDES OF CHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a domestic violence and sexual assault helpline and resource drop-in center. Services include counseling, support groups, advocacy, transportation, health advocacy, rental assistance, accompaniment services, protection order assistance, 24-hour crisis intervention, limited financial assistance, and a hotline. 1902 2nd Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Individuals who are the victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, trafficking, or dating violence Hours: Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 10am-4pm, Wednesday noon-4pm Email (503) 842-9486 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON HOME FREE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a domestic violence and sexual intervention program that includes services such as: -Emergency Services: Hotline, information and referral, and telephone advocacy -Housing Services: Home visits, long-term advocacy and support, help to locate and maintain safe housing -Domestic and sexual violence healthcare and advocacy -Court and legal advocacy with a restraining order hotline -Economic Empowerment: Credit building and repair advocacy, savings, interview skills, employment seekers group, barrier reduction assistance, and Rent Well landlord-tenant education Motel vouchers are only available for those in emergencies who are actively fleeing domestic violence. Availability is dependent upon funding. Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Emergency Hotline: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Restraining Order Services: Monday-Friday 8am-2pm Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 235-8655 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER AGAINST RAPE AND DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT GROUPS CENTER FOR HOPE AND SAFETY SERVICES CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS BEHAVORIAL HEALTH CENTER FOR FAMILIES CLACKAMAS WOMEN'S SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES COMMUNITY WORKS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS CIRCLES OF HEALING CONSEJO HISPANO MUJERES APOYANDO MUJERES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC SUPPORT GROUPS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC SUPPORT GROUPS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC SUPPORT GROUPS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC SUPPORT GROUPS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC SUPPORT GROUPS DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO UNICA GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE HEART OF GRANT COUNTY HEART OF GRANT COUNTY HELPING HANDS AGAINST VIOLENCE HOTLINE AND EMERGENCY SERVICES HENDERSON HOUSE CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES HOPE AND SAFETY ALLIANCE CRISIS LINE AND SUPPORT CENTER ILLINOIS VALLEY SAFE HOUSE ALLIANCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT SERVICES IMPACT NW PARENT CHILD THERAPEUTIC SERVICES KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HEALING CIRCLE DOMESTIC VIOLENCE PROGRAM PEACE AT HOME ADVOCACY CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SHELTER AND SUPPORT SERVICES SABLE HOUSE SABLE HOUSE SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY SUPPORT GROUPS SAFE PROJECT THE SAFE PROJECT SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE SELF ENHANCEMENT INC ANTI DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE PROGRAM SHELTER FROM THE STORM DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES TIDES OF CHANGE TIDES OF CHANGE
